behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification: Correctness is about translation quality (grammar + meaning preservation), which depends on real NMT model judgments. Stubbed outputs would hide this failure mode. Rejected Workflow because the behavior describes a single model decision, not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluation cases against the real translation model/API and assert adequacy/fluency thresholds (or human-rated acceptance) for meaning preservation and grammatical coherence.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/error-handoff logic across modules (API client -> fallback translator -> response path). It does not require real model output quality, and 503 can be injected with a deterministic stub. Rejected System because the behavior text does not require validating a real external HTTP error contract; it only requires handling a 503 condition.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service with a fake API client that returns 503 and a real/local fallback path; verify caller gets a successful translated response (from dictionary) and no surfaced error.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans translator logic plus real SQLite read path (module wiring + local DB). Unit tests of lookup logic alone can miss schema/query/serialization mismatches. Rejected System because local SQLite/FS is explicitly an Integration-level local infrastructure case.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a test SQLite DB with known phrase pairs, invoke fallback translator through service/repository wiring, and assert exact stored translation is returned.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Although edit distance math can be unit-tested, this behavior also requires persistence of the computed metric, i.e., cross-module handoff to storage. Real model judgment is not required because fixed strings suffice. Rejected Agentic due no dependence on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic primary/fallback strings through the quality-tracking path, assert computed distance matches expected value, then read persisted metric from storage to verify write correctness.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: This tests precedence/orchestration and asynchronous side-effect logging across modules. It can be validated with deterministic translator doubles plus readback of logged metric; no claim about model output quality itself. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real external HTTP/API contract checks, only internal behavior and side-effect logging.
behaviors.test_description: With both translators enabled in an integrated test setup, assert response uses primary translator output, then wait/poll and verify background quality-comparison record is written.
